topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sterile white of the lab felt like a cage. Elias stared at the printed report, the stark genetic markers blurring into an incomprehensible mess. His hands, usually steady when he was sketching in his worn leather-bound notebook, were trembling slightly as he traced the lines detailing his biological lineage. A cold, heavy weight settled in his chest, pressing down until it was hard to draw a full breath. He reread the section about the match percentage, the numbers mocking the sudden, seismic shift in his understanding of himself. He didn't look up when Sarah, his adoptive mother, cleared her throat, her voice warm and oblivious. "Are you alright, sweetie?" Elias managed a tight, almost inaudible sound, shoving the paper into his pocket as if it were suddenly radioactive. The silence that followed felt vast, echoing the hollow space opening up inside him.

[story 2]

I sat on the edge of my bed, the room spinning slightly. The DNA results lay spread across my desk, a cruel map of a life I never knew. Every line and marker screamed a history I was supposed to inherit, a secret lineage that suddenly made my own existence feel flimsy, constructed on borrowed foundations. My jaw was clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. I picked up my phone, the screen illuminating the deep shadows under my eyes. I didn't call anyone. Instead, I just sat there, letting the unfamiliar reality wash over me—a profound sense of displacement, a deep ache in my core that no amount of sleep seemed capable of soothing. The air in the apartment felt thick, suffocating.

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights of the clinic hummed an irritating, monotonous tune above me. When the technician finally presented the data, the words struck me not as information, but as an indictment. My gaze remained fixed on the screen, refusing to acknowledge the face of the person I was genetically linked to. A slow, deliberate movement took my hand, moving it to grip the edge of the table until my knuckles turned white. The world seemed muted, the colors draining away until everything was shades of grey. I felt a profound sense of isolation, as if a vast, unseen chasm had opened between who I thought I was and the truth laid bare before me. I simply nodded, a small, jerky motion, and walked out without a word.
